pages.text:
The sky tears

Aeryndra's Prime Gateway screamed once — then the world learned how to layer.

Nine days before the Commons had a name

Roads crossed deserts that had never met forests.

Elara Venn says: The egg is warm. That is enough to keep walking.

Elara Venn thinks: If I name myself hero, I will fail the small thing in my arms.

Stranger says: Take the crown road — safer.

Elara Venn says: Safer for whom?

She shared waybread with a child who had lost a name.

Elara Venn says: Call the creature yours to keep — not yours to own.

Codex echo: Waybread on the First Night

Gateway Hearts

Codex fragment — recovered from the margin.

Gateway Hearts: Hearts were living cores. Stones are travel shards. The Commons Riftstone is a fragment of the Prime — maps of places not yet found.

The Riftstone claims mud

Farmers, healers, and Keepers gathered where stable paths still intersected.

Mira (young) says: Is it… looking at me?

Elara Venn says: It is remembering the world. Help it stay gentle.

The Soft Exodus had closed into eggs. Battle culture would come later.

Village elder says: What do we call you?

Elara Venn says: Courier. Keeper, if the egg agrees.

Secret symbol: FIRST-LIGHT-01

First Riftling thinks: …keep…

Original IP note: Riftlings are living archives — not weapons-first. Preservation before prowess.

Years soft-shifted. Elara's bond slowed her aging — Spirit bleed, not magic vanity.

Elara Venn says: New Keepers still arrive with empty hands and full fear.

Elara Venn says: Good. Fear means they noticed the egg is becoming.

Play this chapter in Live World when you are ready.

The road remembers

Footpaths in the Riftwilds rewrite themselves overnight. Maps are promises, not contracts.

Elara Venn says: Carry water. Carry kindness. Carry the name of whoever waits for you.

Keeper thinks: Credits buy supplies. Courage is not for sale.

Quiet between storms

Between named trials, Keepers learn the oldest skill: waiting without wasting the wait.

Elara Venn says: Carry water. Carry kindness. Carry the name of whoever waits for you.

Keeper thinks: Credits buy supplies. Courage is not for sale.

The road remembers

Footpaths in the Riftwilds rewrite themselves overnight. Maps are promises, not contracts.

Elara Venn says: If the path changes, walk it anyway. The Heart notices who still shows up.

Keeper thinks: Credits buy supplies. Courage is not for sale.

Issue complete

You finished Issue #1: The First Rift.

Elara Venn carries a damaged egg through the Fracture's first nights and names the path of keeping at the Riftstone.

Play this chapter → Enter Live World — Commons

Shape the side-story

What should Elara's next courier route emphasize?

Care first says: More hatchery ethics scenes next arc.

Exploration says: Push toward unmapped Gateway paths.
